title: Syracuse sues company that was supposed to save Skyline Apartments, says they ‘failed to invest’
summary: Syracuse Mayor Ben Walsh's administration filed a lawsuit against Clear Investment Group, a Chicago-based developer who acquired the Skyline Apartments in 2023 with promises to renovate the 352-unit building. Clear has failed to execute its $4.6M renovation plan, and the property has deteriorated significantly including a catastrophic water leak in March 2025 that released an estimated 2 million gallons. The city seeks appointment of a receiver, $2M in repair deposits, and enforcement of $355K in accumulated code violations.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Mayor's administration initiating legal action against private developer for breach of covenant and public health endangerment; signals prioritization of housing conditions and accountability over developer relationships
